question_title: Will the interest in “kc-135” change between 2026-03-16 and 2026-03-28 according to Google Trends?
summary: The KC-135 search term experienced a massive spike (peak 100 on Mar 13) due to a KC-135 Stratotanker crash in Iraq during Operation Epic Fury that killed 6 crew members. The spike has been decaying rapidly: 100→37→19→9 over 3 days. The baseline interest for "kc-135" is 0-1 (essentially zero). By Mar 28 (15 days post-peak), interest should return to near baseline (0-2), creating a decrease of approximately 7-9 points from the Mar 16 value of ~9—well beyond the ±3 threshold. The ongoing US-Iran conflict creates some tail risk of a renewed spike (another KC-135 incident, investigation results, Congressional hearings), but the probability of a KC-135-specific catalyst strong enough to keep search interest above 6 by Mar 28 is low. Monte Carlo simulation with a mixture model (85% baseline return, 10% residual, 5% renewal) yields 87% Decreases. I adjust slightly downward to 77% to account for the active conflict context and SerpAPI measurement uncertainty.

factors.description: Post-spike exponential decay: KC-135 interest spiked to 100 on Mar 13 from Iraq crash, decayed to 9 by Mar 16. By Mar 28 (day 15), should return to baseline 0-2, giving a decrease of ~7-9 well beyond the -3 threshold.
Baseline interest for 'kc-135' is 0-1 (essentially zero). The natural equilibrium after any news spike strongly pulls back to baseline within 7-14 days.
Ongoing US-Iran conflict (Operation Epic Fury) means KC-135s are actively in combat operations. Another incident, investigation results, or Congressional hearings could renew search interest.
SerpAPI vs pytrends measurement differences could shift values by ±2-3 points, potentially affecting threshold crossing at the margin.
Monte Carlo simulation (100K runs, mixture model) yields 87% Decreases, 8% Doesn't change, 5% Increases across all plausible Mar 16 starting values.

reasoning:
**Forecast: KC-135 Google Trends Interest Change (Mar 16–28)**

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|------------|
| **Decreases** | **77%** |
| Doesn't change | 13% |
| Increases | 10% |

**Key reasoning:**
- The "kc-135" search term spiked to 100 on Mar 13 due to a fatal KC-135 crash in Iraq (6 killed during Operation Epic Fury), then decayed rapidly: 100→37→19→9 over 3 days
- Baseline interest is 0-1 (essentially zero before the crash)
- By Mar 28 (day 15 post-peak), interest should return to baseline (0-2), giving a decrease of ~7-9 from the Mar 16 value of ~9 — well past the ±3 threshold
- The ongoing US-Iran conflict provides some tail risk of renewed interest (another incident, investigation results), but specific "kc-135" searches are unlikely to sustain above 6 for 12 more days
- Monte Carlo simulation confirms ~87% Decreases; I adjust to 77% for conflict context and measurement uncertainty
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the interest in “kc-135” change between 2026-03-16 and 2026-03-28 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42687)
resolution_reason: The Google Trends data for "kc-135" in the US (with tz=0) shows that the value on 2026-03-16 was 7 and the value on 2026-03-28 was 3. The difference is -4, meaning the value decreased by more than 3. According to the resolution criteria, this resolves to "Decreases" since the value is more than 3 lower than the reference date.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=kc-135&date=2026-02-26%202026-03-28](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=kc-135&date=2026-02-26%202026-03-28) for 2026-03-28 is within 3 of the value at 2026-03-16. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "kc-135",
  "date": "2026-02-26 2026-03-28",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
